name: 5 Star Car Title Loans
description_short: 5 Star Car Title Loans offers vehicle-secured loans from $100 to $50,000 with same-day funding. Borrowers keep their car while repaying; operating in 12+ states since 2012.
description_long:
5 Star Car Title Loans™ is a licensed title lending company headquartered in San Bernardino, California, that has been operating since 2012. The company reports having funded over 255,000 loans across multiple states and currently serves customers in approximately 12 states including California, Florida, Indiana, Kentucky, Louisiana, Michigan, Ohio, Oklahoma, Tennessee, Texas, Virginia, and Washington.

The company offers three core products: vehicle title loans secured by the borrower's car, motorcycle, or semi-truck; installment loans with fixed monthly payments over periods of up to 24 months; and same-day loans for urgent cash needs. Loan amounts range from $100 to $50,000, determined by the vehicle's equity value. Applications can be completed online or in person at one of their physical locations, and the company advertises approval decisions within minutes and same-day fund disbursement for qualified applicants.

Several features distinguish 5 Star Car Title Loans from pawn-style collateral lenders. Borrowers retain full use of their vehicle throughout the loan term — the title serves as collateral, not the car itself. The company advertises no prepayment penalties, no hidden fees, and a 256-bit SSL/TLS-secured application process. Loans are marketed to applicants with imperfect credit, as underwriting is primarily based on vehicle equity rather than credit score. The company accepts non-standard collateral including motorcycles and commercial semi-trucks, which is less common among title lenders.

As with all car title loans, prospective borrowers should approach this product with clear eyes. Title loans are a high-cost form of secured lending — the company does not disclose interest rates or APR figures on its public website, which is a meaningful transparency gap. Defaulting on a title loan puts your vehicle at risk of repossession regardless of how essential it is to your daily life. The product is best suited for borrowers who have exhausted lower-cost options, own a vehicle outright or with significant equity, and have a concrete repayment plan. The 11-review sample on their own site offers limited independent verification of the advertised experience.
pros: Borrower keeps the vehicle and continues driving throughout the loan term
Loans available up to $50,000 based on vehicle equity, not credit score
Same-day funding available for qualified applicants
No prepayment penalties — pay off early without extra cost
Accepts non-standard collateral including motorcycles and semi-trucks
Licensed and regulated lender with 14 years operating history (since 2012)
255,000+ loans funded — established volume track record
cons: No APR or interest rate information disclosed anywhere on the public website
Vehicle repossession is the consequence of default — high stakes collateral
Only available in approximately 12 states; no service in most of the US
Onsite review base is very thin (11 reviews) with no third-party verification links
Title loans are a high-cost credit product; no lower-rate alternatives mentioned
best_for: Vehicle owners who need fast cash and cannot qualify for traditional personal loans
Borrowers with poor or no credit history who have significant car equity
People facing urgent, short-term cash needs (medical bills, rent, emergency repairs) in the states served
Commercial vehicle owners (semi-truck operators) needing collateral-based financing
diagnosis: 5 Star Car Title Loans is best for vehicle owners with poor credit who need fast cash and have no lower-cost borrowing options available. The main caveat is that the company does not disclose interest rates on its website, which is a red flag for cost transparency, and title loans carry real vehicle repossession risk if the borrower cannot repay.
services: Car title loans (secured by vehicle title)
Installment loans (fixed monthly payments, up to 24 months)
